summary: RETEST PASSED. Portal fix (ReactDOM.createPortal to document.body) for Vibez 654 side-bets popup is verified working. Popup now renders full-screen as designed on both desktop and mobile. Premium /dice popup regression is also intact.

vibez_654_mobile.note: had to dismiss landscape-hint-overlay (game prompts rotate-to-landscape in portrait mobile, unrelated to popup fix). Once dismissed, popup renders perfectly full-screen with inset-0.

premium_dice_regression.result: PASS - Side Bets popup opens full-screen on Premium with all bet types (Triple 6, One & Done, Straight 1s, Straight 6s, Large Straight, Predict Point, Qualify Roll 1/2/3, etc) visible. Premium uses its own implementation (different data-testid), so the v654 portal change had no regression impact.

critical_code_review_comments: Portal implementation is correct: createPortal with document.body guards against SSR via 'typeof document !== "undefined"' check.
z-index 60/70 is appropriate, sits above most overlays (the landscape-hint-overlay is z-57, so popup correctly renders above it on mobile-portrait once dismissed).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Vibez 654 side-bets popup uses ReactDOM.createPortal to document.body (lines 736-768 of Vibez654Game.tsx) to escape the parent Drawer's backdrop-blur-md containing block. Verified working on 1920x1080 (96.7%x94.1% via md:inset-8) and 390x844 (100%x100% via inset-0). Premium /dice popup is a separate component and is unaffected by the v654 change.
rca of the issue: Previously diagnosed: parent Drawer had 'backdrop-blur-md' (a CSS filter), which per CSS Filter Effects spec creates a containing block for fixed-positioned descendants. This caused the 'fixed inset-0' popup to be sized relative to the collapsed 670x2px Drawer instead of the viewport. Fix: portal the overlay to document.body so it is no longer a descendant of the filtered ancestor. Retested and confirmed: popup parent is now BODY and bounding rect matches viewport bounds exactly.
